name: Sugar-Free Vanilla Donut Glaze
description: A pure sweet white glaze for protein timbits/donut holes - the classic thin sugar glaze, made without powdered sugar. No powdered-sugar sub on hand, so granulated Splenda is blitzed to a fine powder for the body, loosened with almond milk, and the kick comes from liquid monk fruit + a pinch of salt + vanilla (concentrated sweetness with no grit). Deliberately FLAVOR-NEUTRAL so it works as the glaze on any timbit flavor - the chocolate (or whatever) lives in the timbit, the glaze is just clean sweet shine. Reusable sub-recipe. 1 batch glazes ~16 timbits.

steps.instructionText: Blitz the granulated Splenda to a fine powder in a blender or spice grinder - this is your DIY powdered sweetener and the body of the glaze. (Granulated sweetener won't dissolve smooth; powdering it is what gives a clean, non-grainy coat.)

Whisk the powdered Splenda with the almond milk, vanilla, liquid monk fruit, and a pinch of salt until smooth and just pourable - it should ribbon off the whisk and coat the back of a spoon. Add almond milk a few drops at a time if too thick. Taste and add monk fruit a drop at a time until the sweetness pops.

notes.text: Powdered sweetener glaze sets softer than a real powdered-sugar glaze - it firms as it dries on a rack and firms more in the fridge, but it stays a thin glaze, not a hard crackable shell. Keep it on the thicker side (less almond milk) and let it dry undisturbed for the best set.

The kick is concentration, not bulk: liquid monk fruit pushes sweetness up with zero grit, and a pinch of salt sharpens it so it reads like a real sugar glaze instead of flat diet-sweet. Always finish by tasting and adjusting the monk fruit.

Flavor-neutral on purpose. To theme it to a flavor without breaking the all-timbit reusability, swap the vanilla for another extract (maple, almond, peppermint) - but for chocolate timbits leave it plain vanilla so the glaze stays the clean sweet contrast to the chocolate.
